name: crate
description: CrateDB is a distributed and scalable SQL database for storing and analyzing massive amounts of data in near real-time, even with complex queries. It is PostgreSQL-compatible, and based on Lucene.

commit.message:
Add support for NULL/(SELECT NULL) within ORDER BY

Closes https://github.com/crate/crate/issues/18108
Add missing contrib info to release notes
Store UDFs in SchemaMetadata instead of custom metadata
Expose stored oid instead of oid hash

Re-enable CORS headers test on blob endpoint

CORS headers are only set on OPTIONS pre-flight requests

See:
- https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Reference/Methods/OPTIONS#preflighted_requests_in_cors
- https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Preflight_request
Add support for IS (NOT) TRUE/FALSE predicates

Add parse_ident scalar function

PostgreSQL-compatible pg_catalog.parse_ident(text [, boolean]) scalar
function that splits a qualified SQL identifier string into an array
of identifiers. Reuses SqlParser's qname rule via invokeParser for
both strict and non-strict identifier parsing.

Fixes https://github.com/crate/crate/issues/18660
Fix buffer leak when HTTP request fails on session creation
Ensure that BlobHttpHandler doesn't leak request that is not being forwarded further

Migrate ForeignTablesMetadata to RelationMetadata.ForeignTable

Remove ForeignTable class, and use RelationMetadata.ForeignTable
also for the Info purposes, by making it implement the `TableInfo`
interface.
Add debug logging for non-IOExceptions shown to users
Add Metadata.oidsRelations for faster RelationMetadata lookup by oids

Replace integration test for KILL with a unit test

If some test is executed in parallel before we release CB bytes in finally, it can cause random flakiness in other tests

Migrate custom ViewsMetadata to RelationMetadata.View

Preparation for DROP SCHEMA ... CASCADE.
It will make it easier to treat views under `Schemas` as we do for the
tables.

Relates: #18794

Use IntArrayList instead of Set for readerIds in reader allocations

The way readerIds are generated (base+nodeEntries) should already ensure
that the readerIds are unique and the deduplication/hashing isn't
necessary.

Remove intermediate Restore.Request

We can instead use the original RestoreSnapshotRequest which has mostly
the same properties.
Reflect 6.2.2 release

Add support for empty statements containing comments

Closes https://github.com/crate/crate/issues/16501

Add ALTER TABLE ALTER COLUMN SET/DROP DEFAULT

Allows modifying or removing column default values after table creation:

  ALTER TABLE t ALTER COLUMN col SET DEFAULT 'value';
  ALTER TABLE t ALTER COLUMN col DROP DEFAULT;

Full-stack implementation across parsing (ANTLR grammar), analysis,
planning, and execution layers. Validates that DEFAULT expressions
are type-compatible with the column. Works on partitioned tables,
sub-columns of OBJECT types, PRIMARY KEY columns, and ARRAY columns.

Use isShardNotAvailable in maybeTemporary

Covers more errors related to shards unavailability

Fix BWC handling in TransportCreatePartitions

Follow up to https://github.com/crate/crate/pull/19029 If
request.tableOID == 0 (unassigned) it doesn't imply that there is a
relation in the master's cluster state where the OID is assigned.

Fixes crate-qa failures like:

    crate.client.exceptions.ProgrammingError: NotSerializableExceptionWrapper[ClassCastException: class org.elasticsearch.cluster.metadata.RelationMetadata$BlobTable cannot be cast to class org.elasticsearch.cluster.metadata.RelationMetadata$Table (org.elasticsearch.cluster.metadata.RelationMetadata$BlobTable and org.elasticsearch.cluster.metadata.RelationMetadata$Table are in unnamed module of loader 'app')]
    org.elasticsearch.common.io.stream.NotSerializableExceptionWrapper: ClassCastException: class org.elasticsearch.cluster.metadata.RelationMetadata$BlobTable cannot be cast to class org.elasticsearch.cluster.metadata.RelationMetadata$Table (org.elasticsearch.cluster.metadata.RelationMetadata$BlobTable and org.elasticsearch.cluster.metadata.RelationMetadata$Table are in unnamed module of loader 'app')
    	at org.elasticsearch.action.admin.indices.create.TransportCreatePartitions.lambda$createIndices$0(TransportCreatePartitions.java:320)

Account for more temporal errors when handling a failure in DistributingConsumer

Follow up to https://github.com/crate/crate/commit/a6139f00b47b91e96dc5d4f2e6b79f3adb4b34d6 and https://github.com/crate/crate/commit/43be3356b9354593271d887c8d8dbc897f2df16a

Remove CollectTask dependency from GenericFunctionQuery

Follow up to https://github.com/crate/crate/commit/a3a848639a6a669542bb0e164b2d997d21f1a88e

Changing directed GC graph from
`GenericFunctionQuery` -> `CollectTask`
to
`GenericFunctionQuery` -> `KillToken` <- `CollectTask`

This way queries in cache don't retain potentially heavy CollectTask instances

Adds contributor information to the release notes

Follow up of #18987 and #18992.
Add regexp_instr scalar #18984

Fix object indexing containing nested arrays of incompatible types

Type guessing of nested arrays with incompatible inner types
raises an exception while the usage inside ``IGNORED`` objects
is allowed.
Only guess such types if no child indexer exists already.

Fixes #19082.
Remove unused HppcMaps
Convert ShardRestoreInfo to record
Replace ObjectHashSet with HashSet

Fixes nested subscript resolving when parents aren't known

- Fixes generic subscript fallback logic, it must use the original
  base instead of the maybe resolved intermediate parent
- Fixes index-based subscript resolving when parent is already a
  generic subscript function
Remove ImmutableOpenMap
Replace ImmutableOpenMap in Metadata
Replace ImmutableOpenMap around RestoreService
Replace ImmutableOpenMap around SnapshotShardSizeInfo
Replace ImmutableOpenMap around ClusterInfo
Replace ImmutableOpenMap around SnapshotsService
Replace ImmutableOpenMap in ClusterBlocks

Replace ImmutableOpenMap in DiscoveryNodes with regular Map

It's unclear if the custom map implementation has any advantages for the
acess pattern in the DiscoveryNodes.

This replaces the ImmutableOpenMap with a regular Map/HashMap with the
goal of removing the ImmutableOpenMap altogether eventually.

(Only the primitive maps like IntMap, etc. should remain. Those have
lower memory footprint)

Ensure that forwardFailure tripped by CB doesn't cause job leaks

Follow up to https://github.com/crate/crate/pull/19049
Fix NPE when SWAP interleaved with INSERT on partitioned table

Don't call DocTableInfoFactory.create in verifyIndexMetadata

The used `create` overload is deprecated and should only be used for BWC
if upgrading older metadata.

`verifyIndexMetadata` is also called in other cases - e.g. altering
table settings.

`.create(IndexMetadata)` is mostly validating the `mapping()` which
should otherwise no longer being used because it has been replaced by
the RelationMetadata.

Relates to https://github.com/crate/crate/pull/19066

Fix handling of columns of type undefined during alter table

Alter table still uses the deprecated
`DocTableInfoFactory.create` overload that uses `IndexMetadata` to
verify the settings after an upgrade.

If the table contained a column with name ``undefined`` it was mapped to
``not_supported`` instead of ``undefined`` due to a missing type mapping
entry. It then failed on ``storageSupport`` leading to an error:

    UnsupportedOperationException: Type NOT SUPPORTED does not support storage

Closes https://github.com/crate/crate/issues/19065
Update junit to 6.0.3

Add DEFAULT keyword support in INSERT Statement (#19053)

Allows explicitly invoking a column's default value in INSERT statements:

  INSERT INTO t (col1, col2) VALUES (DEFAULT, 'explicit');

When DEFAULT is used for a column that has a default expression, that
expression is used. When no default is defined, NULL is inserted.


Co-authored-by: Mathias Fussenegger <f.mathias@zignar.net>

verification.payload:
tree 8abbc316b240fd3226adcb7ef9f1607ee621f49d
parent a4e6d29f8f1baad9365cc2e8fde1ee284838a248
author Dhruv Patel <71836462+DHRUV6029@users.noreply.github.com> 1771834559 -0800
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1771834559 +0100

Add DEFAULT keyword support in INSERT Statement (#19053)

Allows explicitly invoking a column's default value in INSERT statements:

  INSERT INTO t (col1, col2) VALUES (DEFAULT, 'explicit');

When DEFAULT is used for a column that has a default expression, that
expression is used. When no default is defined, NULL is inserted.


Co-authored-by: Mathias Fussenegger <f.mathias@zignar.net>

commit.message: Test: Improve test_streaming_table_oid

Fix MetadataDiff streaming to ensure currentMaxTableOid is monotonically increasing

Follows #18954

Don't trip circuit breaker on KILL ALL requests

Follow up to https://github.com/crate/crate/commit/a6139f00b47b91e96dc5d4f2e6b79f3adb4b34d6

Fix null value handling in topk aggregation

Relates to https://github.com/crate/crate/issues/19042

Fix handling of null values in SUM used as window function

Closes https://github.com/crate/crate/issues/19042

Don't trip circuit breaker on KILL requests

KILL must be always sent in order to not leave behind open tasks.

Change ANALYZE to process tables sequentially

Closes https://github.com/crate/crate/issues/18103
Docs: Fix a couple of minor layout issues in release notes
Update assertj & randomizedrunner
Set junit version via bom
Add regexp_count scalar function

Add a breaker.policy to kill the top consumer instead of current query

If a user runs one heavy query like a join or a group by it can happen
that other queries which would be cheap get rejected with a circuit
breaker exception.
This continues until the heavy query either completes or also trips the
breaker mechanism.

This adds a `indices.breaker.policy` setting which can be set to
`top_consumer` to change the circuit breaker behavior to instead kill
the top consumer instead of the query tripping the breaker. A cheap
query tripping the breaker would then instead kill the heavy query.

In order to resolve some cyclic dependencies this had to change
the TasksService and JobsLogsService wiring a bit.

Adds AI contribution guidelines and AI agent instructions

Content is derived form llama.cpp's AI instructions:
- https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#ai-usage-policy
- https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/blob/master/AGENTS.md
Fix `SWAP TABLE` IllegalStateException due to duplicate table aliases
Report bytesUsed for the CountTask

Don't use bytesUsed supplier in OperationContext

It's not needed as sys.operations is derived from active tasks
starting from https://github.com/crate/crate/commit/f95f6638b9c60211d15462eac896baa68edab45d
and helps to simplify dependencies between components.

Azure Copy Plugin: remove SharedAsyncExecutor from managed components

Starting from https://github.com/crate/crate/commit/b18bd91fb2473bef08979f6244212f2331afafc6,
close() takes care of the resource lifecycle,
so no need to having it in the plugin components list anymore

Reduce CollectingBatchIterator newInstance redirection

Skips a intermediate `newInstance` by calling the constructor directly.
Also re-uses `Exceptions.toRuntimeException` for the runtime exception
handling.

Update Netty to 4.2.10

https://netty.io/news/2026/02/05/4-2-10.html

Replace Stack in WhereClauseValidator with ArrayDeque

`Stack` is synchronized and `ArrayDeque` is generally the recommended
replacement for a stack use case.

Also removes the `Context` wrapper.

Documentation: Fix SyntaxWarning with `sphinx-csv-filter`

<unknown>:1: SyntaxWarning: "\w" is an invalid escape sequence.
Such sequences will not work in the future. Did you mean "\\w"?
A raw string is also an option.

Documentation: Clean up requirements

- `sphinx-autobuild` no longer needs to be pinned in 2026.
- `pygments` versions are also best determined by `sphinx` and friends?

Documentation: Update to sphinx-csv-filter 0.4.3

`sphinx-csv-filter` is not compatible with recent `docutils`.
Now, it says so on its own on behalf of the most recent release.

Fix table SWAP/DROP that corrupt Metadata in a mixed cluster

caused by table deletes streamed in from a node < 6.0 via
MetadataDiff.indices and MetadataDiff.templates that are
NOT applied to Metadata.schemas
Add release notes: 6.0.6, 6.1.4

Exclude wikipedia URLs from linkcheck

Running into 403 errors because of their rate limiting.

Support SELECT with no columns for PostgreSQL compatibility

Add support for SELECT statements without a column list (e.g., SELECT FROM table) to match PostgreSQL behavior. This enables compatibility with tools like TinyETL that use reflection queries such as:

SELECT EXISTS (SELECT FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_name = $1)

verification.payload:
tree 07e2a00fb7eb08cf1bdbbb880c3d63dcb1d9c58c
parent 0237ceff26091ce99f3ff823d0287f6eed6c3fc8
author Dhruv Patel <71836462+DHRUV6029@users.noreply.github.com> 1770795893 -0800
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1770795893 +0100

Support SELECT with no columns for PostgreSQL compatibility

Add support for SELECT statements without a column list (e.g., SELECT FROM table) to match PostgreSQL behavior. This enables compatibility with tools like TinyETL that use reflection queries such as:

SELECT EXISTS (SELECT FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_name = $1)

commit.message:
Fix link to Jepsen tests and rolling upgrade docs anchor

See https://github.com/jepsen-io/jepsen/commit/edac1c6baf12ea264fdf5e8d3ebe6166baab76bb

Set User-Agent header for linkcheck

Daily linkcheck job has been failing with 403's

Maybe setting a User-Agent helps avoid getting blocked by AI-bot/crawler
protection mechanisms

Add the PostgreSQL license notice to 3rd-party-notices

Allowing us to use documentation snippets from the PG code base.

Update 3RD-PARTY-NOTICES

Generated using `./mvnw license:add-third-party` plus some reformatting

Ensure there is only a single AsyncExecutor instance

To avoid spawning additional threads per OpenDAL based plugin

Fix CLA check (use pull_request_target event)

Was reverted in: https://github.com/crate/crate/pull/18993

But should be save according to the docs:

https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/events-that-trigger-workflows#pull_request_target

verification.payload:
tree acd4c018655100b0672da2b6e021f50b6f494ba8
parent e69fc5042951b080b6c70a51d58c96b561b21fae
author Brian Munkholm <brian.munkholm@crate.io> 1770718167 +0100
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1770718167 +0100

Fix CLA check (use pull_request_target event)

Was reverted in: https://github.com/crate/crate/pull/18993

But should be save according to the docs:

https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/events-that-trigger-workflows#pull_request_target

commit.message:
Remove custom OpenDAL thread filter from AzureCopyIntegrationTest

They shouldn't be needed as we close the OpenDAL `AsyncExecutor` when
closing `Node` instances.
Convert PlainBlobMetadata/BlobMetadata to record
Migrate es-repository-azure to OpenDAL

Exclude guava from jwks-rsa

See https://github.com/auth0/jwks-rsa-java/pull/121
jwks-rsa only uses guava for the `GuavaCachedJwkProvider`

We use our own `CachingJwkProvider` instead
Together with https://github.com/crate/crate/pull/18997 this means we
won't need guava on the classpath anymore
Change repository-gcs plugin to use OpenDAL

Add `region` option support for s3 repositories

Required to use alternative s3 implementations like https://garagehq.deuxfleurs.fr/

Revert "Fix: CLA GHA updated to use pull_request_target event (#18991)"

This reverts commit 6e31b1bf5dbcc10195dfbff8ca76f0dd7756b958.

Using `pull_request_target` may leak secrest to user code,
we should consider different solutions instead.
Fix: CLA GHA updated to use pull_request_target event (#18991)

verification.payload:
tree 1aea8974774c2050e1cbd4ae221dcdca480c60b9
parent 3ebb4fcfc2392269444fde9238a22813a9d03ceb
author Brian Munkholm <brian.munkholm@crate.io> 1770388953 +0100
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1770388953 +0100

Fix: CLA GHA updated to use pull_request_target event (#18991)

commit.message: Change es-repository-s3 plugin to use OpenDAL
Introduce table OID
Reflect 6.1.3 and 6.2.1 releases

Update JDK to 25.0.2 in github actions

Follow up to https://github.com/crate/crate/pull/18975

Reformat pom.xml files with indent=4; add indent_size to editorconfig

There was a mix of indent 2 & 4 - this unifies it to 4
Update bundled JDK to 25.0.2

Fix flaky PrivilegesIntegrationTest

Became flaky with https://github.com/crate/crate/pull/18827 due to two
test methods creating the same function without drop function in the
teardown:

    org.postgresql.util.PSQLException:
    ERROR: User defined Function 'my_schema.foo(bigint)' already exists.
      Where: io.crate.expression.udf.UserDefinedFunctionService.putFunction(UserDefinedFunctionService.java:181)
    io.crate.expression.udf.UserDefinedFunctionService$1.execute(UserDefinedFunctionService.java:102)
    org.elasticsearch.cluster.ClusterStateUpdateTask.execute(ClusterStateUpdateTask.java:45)
    org.elasticsearch.cluster.service.MasterService.executeTasks(MasterService.java:664)
    org.elasticsearch.cluster.service.MasterService.calculateTaskOutputs(MasterService.java:281)
    org.elasticsearch.cluster.service.MasterService.runTasks(MasterService.java:176)
    org.elasticsearch.cluster.service.MasterService.lambda$doStart$0(MasterService.java:122)
    org.elasticsearch.cluster.service.TaskBatcher.runIfNotProcessed(TaskBatcher.java:170)
    org.elasticsearch.cluster.service.BatchedTask.run(BatchedTask.java:71)
    org.elasticsearch.common.util.concurrent.PrioritizedEsThreadPoolExecutor$TieBreakingPrioritizedRunnable.runAndClean(PrioritizedEsThreadPoolExecutor.java:208)
    org.elasticsearch.common.util.concurrent.PrioritizedEsThreadPoolExecutor$TieBreakingPrioritizedRunnable.run(PrioritizedEsThreadPoolExecutor.java:171)
    java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1090)
    java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:614)
    java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:1474)
    	at __randomizedtesting.SeedInfo.seed([9A2F090BD8464E65:9F196E470CC6AA77]:0)

Implement has_function_privilege()

Closes: #18814

Add information_schema.view_column_usage table

Closes https://github.com/crate/crate/issues/18002

Don't propagate added undefined type to parent column inner types with concrete types

We started adding UndefinedType sub-columns for null values in 5ec34b2

This needs to be taken into account in child type propagation that was introduced in d7b27a2

Undefined type must not overwrite a concrete type on schema updates, it's handled by DocTableInfo.staleUndefined

Propagation of UndefinedType cancels outs logic to do nothing on staleUndefined.

As a consequence, dynamic mapping update with subcol:null can put an UndefinedType
instead of existing concrete type to the cluster state.

That broken state can arrive to another item that just collected schema updates and was waiting for blocking result of AddColumnRequest. That item is then indexed with incorrect UndefinedType and throw UnsupportedFeatureException

Fix nested subscript lookup on aliased function symbols

All child parts must be used when a direct parent isn't resolvable
but upper parents are, resulting in nested subscript functions.
Otherwise, a wrong sub-column will be used when trying to resolve it,
resulting in an unexpected `ColumnUnknownException` or even worse,
resolving an existing wrong column and such wrong results.

Include column name to the UndefinedType indexing error

Helps debugging

Change cr8-copy-s3 plugin to use OpenDAL

Adds a `libs/opendal` package which contains a generic
`OpenDALFileInput` and `OpenDALFileOutput`

Replaces S3-/AzureFileInput and S3-/AzureFileOutput with these generic variants.

Avoid using reflection in Object-/GeoShapeType size estimation

Follow up to https://github.com/crate/crate/pull/18837 and https://github.com/crate/crate/pull/18839
Do not allow CREATE/DROP reserved schemas

readme_html:
.. image:: docs/_static/crate-logo.svg :alt: CrateDB :target: https://cratedb.com

.. image:: https://github.com/crate/crate/actions/workflows/main.yml/badge.svg :target: https://github.com/crate/crate/actions?query=workflow%3A%22CrateDB+SQL%22

.. image:: https://img.shields.io/badge/docs-latest-brightgreen.svg :target: https://cratedb.com/docs/crate/reference/en/latest/

.. image:: https://img.shields.io/badge/container-docker-green.svg :target: https://hub.docker.com/_/crate/

|

Help us improve CrateDB by taking our User Survey! <https://cratedb.com/user-survey>_

About

CrateDB is a distributed SQL database that makes it simple to store and analyze massive amounts of data in real-time.

CrateDB offers the benefits_ of an SQL database and the scalability and flexibility typically associated with NoSQL databases. Modest CrateDB clusters can ingest tens of thousands of records per second without breaking a sweat. You can run ad-hoc queries using standard SQL_. CrateDB's blazing-fast distributed query execution engine parallelizes query workloads across the whole cluster.

CrateDB is well suited to containerization, can be scaled horizontally using ephemeral virtual machines (e.g., Kubernetes, AWS, and Azure) with no shared state. You can deploy and run CrateDB on any sort of network — from personal computers to multi-region hybrid clouds and the edge_.

Features

  • Use standard SQL_ via the PostgreSQL wire protocol_ or an HTTP API_.

  • Dynamic table schemas and queryable objects provide document-oriented features in addition to the relational features of SQL.

  • Support for time-series data, real-time full-text search, geospatial data types and search capabilities.

  • Horizontally scalable, highly available and fault-tolerant clusters that run very well in virtualized and containerized environments.

  • Extremely fast distributed query execution.

  • Auto-partitioning, auto-sharding, and auto-replication.

  • Self-healing and auto-rebalancing.

  • User-defined functions_ (UDFs) can be used to extend the functionality of CrateDB.

Screenshots

CrateDB provides an Admin UI_:

.. image:: crate-admin.gif :alt: Screenshots of the CrateDB Admin UI

Try CrateDB

Run CrateDB via the official Docker Image_:

.. code-block:: console

sh$ docker run --publish 4200:4200 --publish 5432:5432 --env CRATE_HEAP_SIZE=1g crate '-Cdiscovery.type=single-node'

Or visit the installation documentation_ to see all the available download and install options.

Once you're up and running, head over to the introductory docs. To interact with CrateDB, you can use the Admin UI sql console or the CrateDB shell_ CLI tool. Alternatively, review the list of recommended clients and tools_ that work with CrateDB.

For container-specific documentation, check out the CrateDB on Docker how-to guide_ or the CrateDB on Kubernetes how-to guide_.

Contributing

This project is primarily maintained by Crate.io_, but we welcome community contributions!

See the developer docs_ and the contribution docs_ for more information.

Security

The CrateDB team and community take security bugs seriously. We appreciate your efforts to responsibly disclose_ your findings, and will make every effort to acknowledge your contributions.

If you think you discovered a security flaw, please follow the guidelines at SECURITY.md_.

Help

Looking for more help?

  • Try one of our beginner tutorials, how-to guides, or consult the reference manual_.

  • Check out our support channels_.

  • Crate.io_ also offers CrateDB Cloud, a fully-managed CrateDB Database as a Service (DBaaS). The CrateDB Cloud Tutorials will get you started.
